political.s: <cite index="2-3,2-4">Nestlé is a publicly traded company with ticker NESN</cite>. FEC data shows bipartisan political donations ($20K raised, 59% Republican / 41% Democratic), indicating balanced engagement across the political spectrum.

charity.s: <cite index="31-4">Nestlé donated 9.2 million kilos of food in 2024 through partner networks across more than 100 food banks</cite>. <cite index="36-1,36-4">The company operates a "Dollars for Doers" program that matches volunteer time with grants to charities where employees volunteer 20 or more hours per year</cite>. Focus areas include food security, disaster relief, and community health initiatives.
charity.amt: Estimated $50-100M+ annually in global charitable giving
charity.focus: Food security, disaster relief, community health, youth development

environment.s: <cite index="20-19">In 2025 Nestlé became part of the CDP A-list with an "A" for climate change</cite>. <cite index="11-4">The company met its 20% GHG reduction target a year early with an 18.59% gross reduction through operational and supply chain changes</cite>. However, <cite index="11-2,11-7">the company missed its deforestation-free target by 3.3 percentage points and is at 87% packaging recyclability against a 100% commitment</cite>.

labor.s: <cite index="22-1">Nestlé has faced $20.3M in regulatory penalties since 2000</cite>. <cite index="30-5,30-6,30-7">The company ranks fifth out of 38 companies in disclosing forced labor policies, improved disclosure on palm oil suppliers, and has strong commitments to addressing forced labor in supply chains</cite>. <cite index="21-4,21-5,21-6">Nestlé has been a project partner on the Fair Labor Association's Harvesting the Future project since 2019, seeking to eliminate hazardous work for workers under 18 and strengthen responsible recruitment</cite>.

dei.s: <cite index="72-1,72-2">Nestlé has a Diversity, Equity & Inclusion rating of 4.2 out of 5 stars based on 7,716 employee ratings, 24.6% higher than average for the Manufacturing sector</cite>. <cite index="73-1">Globally, Nestlé efforts are centred in four focus areas: gender balance, people with disabilities, LGBTQI+ community and race & ethnicity</cite>. <cite index="74-4">Nestlé USA has reached 1:1 pay equity for women and underrepresented minorities</cite>.

animals.s: <cite index="43-1,43-3">Nestlé does not use animal testing to develop conventional foods and drinks such as coffee, tea, cereal and chocolate</cite>. <cite index="43-4,43-5">However, in certain cases it is necessary to carry out animal tests when national authorities require new food ingredients to be tested for safety, and the company uses testing as little as possible</cite>. <cite index="50-1,50-3">Cruelty Free International exposed cruel animal tests carried out by Nestlé causing suffering to dogs, mice, hamsters, rats and pigs</cite>.

guns.s: Not applicable - Nestlé is a food and beverage company with no involvement in firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: <cite index="61-11,61-12">All Nestlé Group companies comply with a mandatory Global Privacy Program comprising internationally recognized privacy principles and designed to evolve with regulatory changes</cite>. <cite index="62-10,62-11">Nestlé shares certain consumer data with platforms like Meta and Google for advertising purposes to measure effectiveness and improve marketing</cite>. <cite index="61-15">The European Union's GDPR continues to set the standard for how standards are developed and implemented</cite>.

execPay.s: Nestlé CEO compensation involves complex structures with base salary, bonuses, and long-term incentive plans. Specific pay ratios are not disclosed in available sources. Executive compensation appears market-competitive but detailed CEO-to-worker ratio data is not publicly disclosed in consistent format.

fec.committeeName: NESTLE PURINA PETCARE COMPANY POLITICAL ELECTION TEAM

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
